details: The "Skill Upgradation Program Karnataka" scheme aims to upgrade the skills of artisans in leather technology, footwear/leather goods making, and designing by sending them for advanced training at renowned institutions like CFTI Chennai, CLRI, and Karnataka Institute of Leather Technology. The program enhances artisans’ skills, allowing them to contribute to the development of the leather industry through advanced training.
benefits: The corporation covers the entire cost of the course for selected artisans.
A stipend is provided during the course duration to support the participants
eligibility: The applicant should be leather artisans.

The applicant should belongs to sub-castes Arundathiyar, Chammadiya, Chamar, Chambar, Chamagar, Madar, Madig, Madiga, Mini Madig, Jambavalu, Haralayya, Machigar, Mochigar, Mochi, muchi, Telugu Mochi, kamaati mocha, Rohidas, Dhor, kakkayya, Kankayya, Samagara, Samagar, Aadhi Anddra, Aadhi Dravida, Aadhi Karnataka among schedule castes.
The applicant should have passed a Secondary School Leaving Certificate (SSLC)/Pre-University Courses (PUC)/Degree to apply for the program.
The age of the applicant should be 18 years.
The applicant's annual family income should not exceed ₹30,000/- in rural areas and ₹25,000/- in urban areas.
The applicant or their family members must not have received benefits from other schemes, except training programs.
The applicant's family members should not be employed in government or semi-government organizations.

online: Choose "New Users Register Here" and fill in your Aadhaar Number and the Captcha code.
An OTP will be sent to your Aadhaar-linked mobile number. Enter the OTP to continue.
Once your DigiLocker account is created, click "Allow" to proceed.
Fill out your email ID, mobile number, create a password, and enter the captcha, then click "Submit".
You will receive OTPs on both your email and mobile number. Enter both OTPs and select "Validate".
Once validated, a confirmation message will indicate your registration is successful.
Select department "Social Welfare Department" and click on the scheme "Skill Upgradation Program"
Click "Apply Online" and enter your registered email ID/mobile number and the OTP.
Select the scheme and provide your personal, address, and banking details as required.
Once submitted, you will receive an Application ID. You can use this ID to track your application status on the portal.

faqs.question: What is the objective of the Skill Upgradation Program?
faqs.answer: The program aims to develop human resources in leather technology to enhance the leather industry.

faqs.question: Who is eligible for the Skill Upgradation Program?
faqs.answer: Youth from artisan communities who have passed SSLC, PUC, or Degree programs are eligible to apply.

faqs.question: What types of courses are covered under the program?
faqs.answer: Short-term and long-term courses in leather technology, footwear and leather goods manufacturing, and design, offered by CFTI/CLRI Chennai and Karnataka Institute of Leather Technology.

faqs.question: What are the benefits provided under the Skill Upgradation Program?
faqs.answer: The entire course fee is covered by the corporation, and participants also receive a stipend during the course.

faqs.question: Is there any fee for the training?
faqs.answer: No, the program is fully sponsored by the corporation.

faqs.question: How can one apply for the Skill Upgradation Program?
faqs.answer: Applicants can apply online through the Seva Sindhu portal by selecting the appropriate scheme under the Social Welfare Department.

faqs.question: What is the process for applying to the Skill Upgradation Program?
faqs.answer: After registering on the Seva Sindhu portal, applicants can apply by filling out their personal details and uploading the required documents.

faqs.question: Are there any age restrictions for this program?
faqs.answer: Applicants must be 18 years or older to apply.

faqs.question: Is there a preference for specific categories under this scheme?
faqs.answer: Yes, 33% of the seats are reserved for female candidates and 5% for physically challenged candidates.

faqs.question: Can applicants from all areas apply?
faqs.answer: Yes, the program is open to eligible candidates from both rural and urban areas.

faqs.question: What happens after applying for the program?
faqs.answer: After submitting the application, the District Level Selection Committee will review and approve eligible applicants.

faqs.question: How are candidates selected for the Skill Upgradation Program?
faqs.answer: The district coordinator compiles a list of eligible candidates, which is approved by the District Level Selection Committee.

faqs.question: How can applicants track their application status?
faqs.answer: Applicants can track their application status using the Application ID on the Seva Sindhu portal.
